activity_fc.name: I want to register for a group activity/fitness class
activity_fc.pdf_box: Register for a PEAK fitness class.

full.text: Building regular physical activity into your routine can improve energy, mood, and overall health. Finding something you enjoy can make it easier to stay consistent.

Fitness classes offer a structured and engaging way to get started, with options available both in-person and virtually. Being part of a class can also provide accountability and group support, which can make it easier to stay motivated over time.
Whether you're new to exercise or looking to stay active, you can choose from a variety of classes that fit your schedule and preferences.
Options include:
PEAK Health and Fitness - employee-only classes offered virtually and across campus
Student Life Center - group fitness classes available with membership
Exploring different classes can help you find an activity you enjoy and keep coming back to.
CLICK to view options and sign up:
PEAK Health and Fitness
Student Life Center

activity_pt.name: I want to schedule a free 1:1 Personal Training session
activity_pt.pdf_box: Schedule your free personal training session.

full.text: You chose to schedule a free Personal Training session.
Getting started or staying consistent with physical activity can be challenging. A structured session can help you build confidence and find a routine that works for you.
In this 60-minute virtual or in-person session, a PEAK personal trainer will provide guidance tailored to your fitness level and goals.
In your session, you can:
Learn proper form and technique
Explore different types of workouts
Build a routine that fits your schedule
Gain confidence in your approach to exercise
This can be a helpful first step in building a consistent, sustainable routine.
Click HERE to schedule your free Personal Training session.

activity_ep.name: I want to schedule a free1:1  Exercise Prescription to create a plan

full.text: Getting started with physical activity can feel overwhelming, especially if you're not sure where to begin. Even small increases in movement can improve energy, mood, and overall health.
A free Exercise Prescription session can help you take that first step with a plan that feels realistic and manageable.

This 60-minute session (available in-person or virtually) is designed to help you move safely and build confidence. Our experts will work with you to create a personalized plan based on your current fitness level and goals.
In your session, you'll:
Receive a customized exercise plan
Learn proper form and technique
Explore activities that fit your lifestyle
Build confidence to stay active over time
Starting with a clear plan can make it easier to stay consistent.
Click HERE to schedule your free Exercise Prescription

activity_bab.name: I want to participate in a group class to improve bone health
activity_bab.pdf_box: Register for Build a Bone to improve bone health.

full.text: Building strength and maintaining bone health are important parts of staying active and reducing injury risk over time. The Build-a-Bone program focuses on helping you improve bone strength, balance, and movement through guided, practical strategies.
Through a series of classes (available in-person or virtually), you'll learn how to move safely, strengthen key muscle groups, and build habits that support long-term bone health.
If this feels like a good fit, you can explore upcoming sessions and get started.
Click HERE to register.

activity_red_butte.name: I want to walk regularly at Red Butte Garden
activity_red_butte.pdf_box: Go for a walk at Red Butte Garden. Visit 10 times and earn WellU credit.

full.text: You chose to take a walk at Red Butte Garden.
Walking is a simple way to support your physical and mental well-being. Regular movement can help improve energy, reduce stress, and support overall health.
As part of the WellU Program, walking at Red Butte Garden can count toward your wellness goals while also providing an opportunity to spend time outdoors.
Swipe your UCard 10 times when you visit Red Butte Garden to earn WellU credit toward your insurance premium reduction.
Click HERE to learn more about Red Butte Garden hours and events.

activity_hc.name: I want to schedule a free 1:1 Health Coaching session
activity_hc.pdf_box: Schedule your free health coaching session.

full.text: You chose to schedule a Health Coaching session to support your physical activity.

Regular movement can improve energy, mood, and overall health-but getting started or staying consistent can be challenging. A Health Coach can help you build a plan that feels realistic and fits your routine.
Our expert Health Coaches offer one-on-one guidance tailored to your goals and lifestyle, available as both an in-person or virtual appointment.
With PEAK Health Coaching, you'll get:
Personalized goal setting and action plans
Ongoing motivation and accountability
Strategies to build sustainable activity habits
Support every step of the way
Click HERE to schedule your free Health Coaching session.

activity_toolkit.name: I want to make a plan on my own
activity_toolkit.pdf_box: Make your movement plan with the interactive activity planner.

full.text: You chose to make your own plan to improve your physical activity.

Regular movement supports heart health, energy levels, and overall well-being. Finding activities that fit your interests, schedule, and current fitness level can make it easier to stay active over time.
To help you get started, use this interactive activity planner that can help you identify activities you enjoy, set goals, and create a plan that works for your lifestyle.
As you build your plan, remember that even small amounts of activity can add up over time. Health guidelines recommend at least 150 minutes of moderate physical activity each week.
Click HERE to access the Physical Activity Planner.

activity_g_action.name: Fitness assessment
activity_g_action.pdf_box: Schedule a fitness assessment to learn even more about your fitness.

full.text: Your responses to the physical activity questions show that you take exercise seriously!
As a "healthy recommendation" we invite you to schedule a PEAK Fitness Assessment.
A PEAK Fitness Assessment is a great way to evaluate your current fitness level and set meaningful, personalized goals for the future.
During this 60-minute session, we'll assess key areas of your fitness, including:
Cardiovascular endurance
Muscular strength and endurance
Flexibility
Body composition
You'll receive a detailed report comparing your results to age-based norms, along with expert recommendations to help you improve or maintain your fitness.
Click HERE to schedule your PEAK Fitness Assessment.

activity_noaction.name: PEAK Resources
activity_noaction.pdf_box: When you're ready, learn about physical activity resources.

full.text: You chose not to take action to improve your physical activity today.

Whether you're building a routine or trying something new, there are options to fit different needs, interests, and experience levels.

anx_help_eap_bhn.name: I want help finding a mental health provider

full.text: You asked for help finding a Mental Health provider.
Life can be challenging, and it's okay to ask for help. Seeking emotional support is a powerful step toward healing, growth, and greater well-being.
Whether you're feeling overwhelmed, stuck, anxious, or simply need someone to talk to, connecting with a mental health professional can help you:
Gain clarity and emotional balance
Build resilience and coping strategies
Improve relationships and self-understanding
Feel more supported and less alone
HMHI Behavioral Health Network (BHN) can help you find a mental health provider that fits your individual preferences.
SupportLinc, employee assistance program, can help connect you to the care you need in a way that fits you. Use group code: uofu to access.
Click HERE for the HMHI Behavioral Health Network provider search tool
Click HERE for SupportLinc

anx_eap.name: I want to talk to a therapist about my Anxiety/Worry
anx_eap.pdf_box: Schedule an appointment through SupportLinc using group code: uofu

full.text: Ongoing anxiety or worry can affect sleep, focus, and overall well-being. Talking with a therapist can help you better understand what you're experiencing and develop strategies to manage it.
SupportLinc provides free, confidential access to licensed therapists and support resources.
Click HERE to visit SupportLinc and schedule an appointment (Use group code: uofu).

anx_pcp.name: I want to work on this area with my primary care provider
anx_pcp.pdf_box: Work with your primary care provider to address your anxiety/worry.

full.text: You chose to work with your Primary Care Provider to address your anxiety or worry.

Your primary care provider can help you understand your symptoms, explore treatment options, and create a plan to support your emotional well-being. They can also connect you with mental health resources or make referrals if additional support is needed.
Don't have a provider or looking for someone new? Our Primary Care Navigation Team can help you find the right fit.
Click HERE to find a Primary Care Provider.

anx_lmc.name: I want to learn about a Lifestyle Medicine Consultation with a healthcare provider
anx_lmc.pdf_box: Learn more about a Lifestyle Medicine Consultation.

full.text: You chose to learn more about the Lifestyle Medicine Consultation.
Stress, sleep, and daily routines can all influence anxiety and overall mental well-being. A lifestyle medicine approach can help you build strategies that support balance and resilience over time.

Lifestyle Medicine is a medical specialty that utilizes lifestyle interventions-healthy eating, physical activity, stress management, optimizing sleep, boosting social connection, and avoiding risky substances-to prevent, treat and reverse chronic disease.
During your consultation, you and your provider will review these areas and create a personalized plan to support both your physical and emotional health.
Click HERE to learn more and request your Lifestyle Medicine Consultation.

anx_self_eap_bhn.name: I want to make my own plan to address my Mental Health
anx_self_eap_bhn.pdf_box: Use SupportLinc to make your own plan. (group code: uofu)

full.text: You chose to make your own plan to address your emotional well-being.
Taking the initiative to create your own plan for better health is a meaningful step. We know that change can feel overwhelming at times, and you don't have to do it all at once.
To support your journey, we've gathered some helpful resources you can explore whenever you're ready-at your own pace, in your own way.
For 24/7 access to licensed therapists and online resources, connect with SupportLinc, employee assistance program, using group code: uofu.
To search for local in-network therapists that meet your needs and preferences, use the provider search tool below.
Click HERE for SupportLinc
Click HERE for the HMHI Behavioral Health Network provider search tool

anx_mindfulness.name: I want to learn about available mindfulness classes

full.text: You chose to register for a Mindfulness Class.
Mindfulness can help you manage anxiety by increasing awareness of your thoughts and building skills to respond more calmly in the moment.
With both in-person and virtual options available, these classes offer practical techniques to help you slow down, stay present, and build a consistent mindfulness practice.
Options include evidence-based programs like Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), along with beginner-friendly and workplace-focused classes.
Each class is guided by experienced instructors and provides a supportive environment to help you build skills over time.
Click HERE to register for a Mindfulness class.

anx_g_action.name: Mindful awareness tools
anx_g_action.pdf_box: Explore mindfulness tools that support your wellbeing.

full.text: Great job taking steps to manage your anxiety or worry. You scored low risk in this area.
Using tools like mindfulness, breathing exercises, or taking short breaks can help you continue to maintain balance and resilience over time.
Even a few minutes of these practices each day can make a meaningful difference.
Click HERE to explore some mindfulness resources that may be helpful.

anx_noaction.name: Resiliency toolkit
anx_noaction.pdf_box: When you're ready, learn about resources to support your wellbeing.

full.text: You chose not to take action for anxiety or worry today.

We understand that taking steps to address emotional well-being can be deeply personal-and sometimes, the timing just isn't right. Sometimes the first step to addressing your health is simply giving yourself time.
If you decide to revisit this in the future, support is available when you're ready.
In the meantime, click HERE to access a resiliency toolkit and explore some resoruces.

anx_noansw.name: EAP
anx_noansw.pdf_box: Find supportive resources through SupportLinc. (group code: uofu)

full.text: It looks like you didn't choose a resource for your mental wellbeing - and that's okay.  Your emotional well-being is just as important as your physical well-being. It affects how you think, feel, and act-and influences every part of your life, from relationships and work to sleep and self-esteem.
To ensure that you have the support that you need, SupportLinc, our new employee assistance program provider, is always available if you ever need help.
Whether you're dealing with stress, anxiety, relationship issues, grief, or work-life balance, SupportLinc provides:
24/7 access to licensed counselors
Short-term, solution-focused therapy
Legal, financial, and family support resources
Online self-guided tools and wellness content
Click HERE to explore what SupportLinc has to offer. (Use group code: uofu)

bmi_nc.name: I want to schedule a free  1:1 Nutrition Consultation
bmi_nc.pdf_box: Schedule your free PEAK nutrition consultation.

full.text: You chose to schedule a free Nutrition Consultation.
Nutrition plays an important role in weight and overall health. Small, sustainable changes can make a meaningful difference over time.
In-Person & Virtual options available.
In this 60-minute one-on-one session, a dietitian will help you create a personalized nutrition plan that fits your lifestyle, preferences, and goals.
What you can expect:
Practical, sustainable strategies tailored to you
Guidance rooted in your real-life routines-not rigid meal plans
Support to help you build long-term, healthy habits
A focus on steady progress, not perfection
Click HERE to schedule your free Nutrition Consultation.

bmi_noom.name: I want to learn more about an online Weight Management program
bmi_noom.pdf_box: Learn more about a Weight Management program.

full.text: You chose to learn more about a Weight Management Program.
Virtual (online) offering

You can register for weight management, administered through Noom. This program is more than just losing weight-it's about building sustainable habits for a healthier lifestyle. Noom combines psychology, personalized coaching, and science-backed tools to help you understand your behaviors, make mindful choices, and stay motivated. With daily lessons, progress tracking, and a supportive community, Noom empowers you to take control of your health-one small, achievable step at a time.
Click HERE to learn more about Weight Management or call 801-213-8720.

It looks like you are a healthy weight for your size.
As a healthy recommendation, you may consider volunteering at the Campus Garden.

Body weight is influenced by long-term habits, including how we eat, move, and engage with our environment. The Edible Campus Gardens program provides a hands-on way to stay active, learn about growing healthy foods, and build skills that can support overall well-being.
You can participate in volunteer sessions, help grow fresh produce, and learn simple gardening skills, no experience needed. Volunteer for 5 hours to earn WellU credit.
If this feels like a meaningful way to support your health, click HERE to explore volunteer opportunities and get involved.

gen_ilp.name: I want to register for a 12 week group-based lifestyle program - Intensive Lifestyle Program (ILP)
gen_ilp.pdf_box: Register for the Intensive Lifestyle Program (ILP).

full.text: You chose to register for the Intensive Lifestyle Program (ILP).
The Intensive Lifestyle Program is a 10-week group program designed to help you build lasting habits that support your overall health and energy. Classes are offered both in-person and virtually.
In this group-based program, you'll focus on key areas that influence long-term health:
Movement - Build a consistent and sustainable activity routine
Nutrition - Learn how to fuel your body in a way that works for you
Stress Management - Develop tools to feel more balanced and in control
Each week, you'll gain practical strategies, expert guidance, and the support of a group-helping you turn small changes into lasting habits.
Click HERE to learn more and register for the Intensive Lifestyle Program (ILP).

full.text: You chose to make your own plan to address your diabetes risk.
Blood sugar can change gradually over time, often without noticeable symptoms. Understanding the factors that influence blood sugar can help you make informed decisions about your health.
The Just the Facts About Prediabetes video series provides practical information about diabetes risk and lifestyle habits that support healthy blood sugar levels.
Click HERE to access the Just the Facts About Prediabetes video series.

meta_dpp.name: I want to register for a 24-session group program to prevent type 2 diabetes
meta_dpp.pdf_box: Register for the National Diabetes Prevention Program (NDPP).

full.text: Your results suggest your A1C may be elevated, which can increase your risk for developing type 2 diabetes over time. The good news is that early steps can make a meaningful difference.
The National Diabetes Prevention Program (NDPP) is an evidence-based, year-long program designed to help you prevent or delay type 2 diabetes through practical, sustainable changes.
With support from a trained lifestyle coach and a group setting (available in-person or virtually), you'll learn how to:
Make realistic changes to your eating habits
Build physical activity into your routine
Manage stress and stay motivated
Create habits that support long-term health
Many participants see improvements in blood sugar, weight, and overall health.
If this feels like a helpful next step, you can learn more and register below.
Click HERE to learn more about the National Diabetes Prevention Program (NDPP).

Lifestyle factors like nutrition, physical activity, and stress play an important role in metabolic health. The Intensive Lifestyle Program is a 10-week group program designed to help you build habits that support these areas over time. Sessions are offered both in-person and virtually.
In this group-based program, you'll focus on:
Movement - Build a consistent and sustainable activity routine
Nutrition - Learn how to fuel your body in a way that supports your health
Stress Management - Develop tools to feel more balanced and in control
Each week, you'll gain practical strategies, expert guidance, and group support-helping you make steady, sustainable progress.
Click HERE to learn more and register for the Intensive Lifestyle Program (ILP).

full.text: It looks like you are doing well in this area.

As a healthy recommendation, you may consider taking a walk at Red Butte Garden as a simple way to stay active and maintain your routine.

As part of the WellU Program, visits to Red Butte Garden can also contribute toward your wellness goals. Swipe your UCard 10 times when you visit Red Butte Garden to earn WellU credit toward your insurance premium reduction.

nutr_nc.name: I want to schedule a free 1:1 Nutrition Consultation
nutr_nc.pdf_box: Schedule your free PEAK nutrition consultation.

full.text: You chose to schedule a free Nutrition Consultation.
Making changes to your nutrition can feel overwhelming, but small, consistent adjustments can lead to meaningful improvements in your health.

In this 60-minute one-on-one session, a dietitian will help you create a personalized plan that fits your preferences, goals, and daily routine. Appointments are available both in-person and virtually.
What you can expect:
Practical, sustainable nutrition strategies tailored to you
Guidance rooted in your real-life routines
Support to build long-term, healthy habits
A focus on progress, not perfection
Click HERE to schedule your free Nutrition Consultation.

nutr_toolkit.name: I want to learn and make my own plan with a self-paced nutrition course

full.text: Learning how to eat well doesn't have to be complicated or expensive. This free, self-paced online course provides practical guidance on building healthy eating habits while staying within your budget.

Through short, interactive lessons, you'll learn how to plan meals, shop smarter, use foods you already have, and make informed choices using nutrition labels. The course also includes tips for staying active and overcoming common barriers.
If you're looking for a flexible way to build skills and confidence around nutrition, this can be a helpful place to start.
Click HERE to get started.

nutr_nw.name: I want to register for a group-based, interactive Nutrition Workshop

full.text: You chose to register for a Nutrition Workshop.
Making changes to how you eat can feel overwhelming, but practical, hands-on experiences can make it easier to build sustainable habits.
During our hands-on Nutrition Workshops, you'll learn how to create simple, nutritious meals and incorporate healthy ingredients into your daily routine.
What you'll get:
Hands-on cooking experiences
Easy, nutritious recipes to try at home
Practical tips for building sustainable eating habits
A variety of rotating topics to keep things engaging
These sessions are designed to help you take small, realistic steps toward healthier eating.
Click HERE to check out our current Nutrition Workshops and register.

nutr_ie.name: I want to register for a group-based Intuitive Eating class
nutr_ie.pdf_box: Register for an Intuitive Eating class.

full.text: You chose to explore an Intuitive Eating class.
Available in-person and virtually, Intuitive Eating helps you build a healthy relationship with food, movement, and self-care-an important part of overall well-being.
In this class, you'll learn to:
Enjoy food without guilt
Move your body to feel good
Respect and appreciate your body
Let go of diet rules
Nourish yourself with kindness
Backed by decades of research, Intuitive Eating is linked to better body image and self-confidence, more satisfaction with food, greater self-compassion and less anxiety, guilt, and binge eating.
If this feels like a good fit, you can register below.
Click HERE to register for an Intuitive Eating class.

Your primary care provider can help you understand how your eating habits affect your health and guide you toward realistic, sustainable changes. They can also connect you with additional resources, such as a dietitian, if needed.

nutr_lmc.name: I want to learn about the Lifestyle Medicine Consultation with a healthcare provider

full.text: You chose to learn more about the Lifestyle Medicine Consultation.
Nutrition plays a key role in energy, metabolism, and overall health. Small, consistent changes to how you eat can have a meaningful impact over time.

Lifestyle Medicine is a medical specialty that utilizes lifestyle interventions-healthy eating, physical activity, stress management, optimizing sleep, boosting social connection, and avoiding risky substances-to prevent, treat and reverse chronic disease.
During your consultation, you'll review your current eating patterns and work with a provider to develop a personalized plan that fits your preferences, goals, and daily routine.
Click HERE to learn more and request your Lifestyle Medicine Consultation.

phq_help_eap_bhn.name: I would like help finding a mental health provider

full.text: You asked for help finding a Mental Health provider.
Life can be challenging, and it's okay to ask for help. Seeking emotional well-being support is a powerful step toward healing, growth, and greater well-being.
Whether you're feeling overwhelmed, stuck, anxious, or simply need someone to talk to, connecting with a mental health professional can help you:

sleep_cbt_i.name: I want to register for a group-based virtual insomnia class
sleep_cbt_i.link: Alert sent to Wellness Team to submit order for insomnia class
sleep_cbt_i.pdf_box: A member of our team will reach out about a virtual insomnia class.

full.text: You chose to register for the Virtual Insomnia class. This is a 5-week virtual group treatment where you'll be asked to keep a sleep diary, and to make adjustments to your sleep schedule and sleep habits throughout the group.

The CBT-I group is intended for adults with insomnia, especially for those with difficulties that have lasted several months or more.  These difficulties can include problems falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up too early, that negatively impact your quality of life.

This treatment can work well for adults with other physical health or mental health conditions, and for people already being treated for other sleep conditions such as Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA) or Restless Leg Syndrome (RLS).

If you're wondering if you have one of these sleep conditions, such as OSA or RLS, but haven't yet been diagnosed or treated for it, please talk to your primary care provider before signing up for this group.
A member of our team will contact you to schedule this class soon.

sleep_cbt_i_app.name: I want to use a free insomnia coach app

full.text: You chose to interact with  the Insomnia Coach app to help improve your sleep. This app is designed for individuals experiencing insomnia who want to build healthier sleep habits using a structured, self-guided approach.

Insomnia Coach is based on Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-i) and includes a training plan, sleep diary, and tools to help you understand your sleep patterns and make gradual changes over time.
It can be used on its own or alongside care from a provider and may be helpful if you have difficulty falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking too early.
This app is not a replacement for medical care. If you have or suspect a sleep condition such as sleep apnea or restless leg syndrome, please talk to your provider before getting started.
Click HERE to download the Insomnia Coach app.

sleep_toolkit.pdf_box: Create your own plan. Here are some useful resources to get started.

Sleep is closely connected to energy, mood, and overall health. Poor or inconsistent sleep can affect multiple areas of well-being.

Lifestyle Medicine is a medical specialty that utilizes lifestyle interventions-healthy eating, physical activity, stress management, optimizing sleep, boosting social connection, and avoiding risky substances-to prevent, treat and reverse chronic disease.
During your consultation, you'll explore factors that may be affecting your sleep and develop a plan to improve sleep habits and daily routines in a sustainable way.

sleep_g_action.name: sleep tools
sleep_g_action.pdf_box: Learn more about getting an even better night's sleep.

full.text: It looks like sleep is going well for you!
Sleep is a vital part of your health and performance. Just like exercise and nutrition, quality sleep is essential for energy, focus, mood, and long-term well-being.

Improving your sleep fitness means creating healthy habits that support deep, restorative rest. From setting a consistent bedtime to creating a calming nighttime routine, small changes can lead to big benefits.
Click HERE for tips and tools for ways to get an even better night's sleep.

sleep_noaction.name: sleep tools

full.text: You chose not to take action to address your sleep today.
We get that it can be hard to make changes-especially when it comes to sleep. Whether it's a busy schedule, stress, or just not knowing where to start, you're not alone.
While you chose not to take action right now, improving sleep can have a big impact on your energy, focus, and overall health. When you're ready, even small steps can make a meaningful difference.
When you are ready to get started, we have put together some resources to help you get a better nights sleep.
Click HERE for resources and tools for better sleep.

full.text: You chose to schedule a free Leisure consultation.
In-Person and Virtual options

Resources in this toolkit provide practical information and strategies to help you better understand your patterns, explore options for change, and build a plan that fits your goals.

As you move forward, these resources can support you in taking next steps at your own pace. If you find that you'd like additional support at any point, know that there are programs and professionals available to help.

Substance use can have a range of effects on your health over time, including energy, sleep, mood, and long-term risk. Making changes in this area can support overall well-being and reduce future health concerns.

Lifestyle Medicine is a medical specialty that utilizes lifestyle interventions-healthy eating, physical activity, stress management, optimizing sleep, boosting social connection, and avoiding risky substances-to prevent, treat, and reverse chronic disease.
During your consultation, you'll work with a provider to explore your current habits and develop a personalized plan that supports your goals and long-term health.

substance_quitline.name: I want information about the Utah Quit line (tobacco use)
substance_quitline.pdf_box: Learn more about Way to Quit and the Utah Tobacco Quit Line.

full.text: You may find resources helpful to address Tobacco/Nicotine use at the Utah Quit line.
Quitting tobacco/nicotine is one of the best decisions you can make for your health-and with Way to Quit and the Utah Tobacco Quit Line, you don't have to do it alone.

Way to Quit offers free, confidential tools to help you break free from nicotine-on your terms. Whether you prefer online support, text coaching, or a personalized quit plan, you'll find the resources you need to succeed at waytoquit.org.
Prefer to talk it out? Call the Utah Tobacco Quit Line at 1-800-QUIT-NOW for one-on-one support from trained quit coaches.
You'll get expert guidance, encouragement, and even access to free nicotine replacement therapies.
Click HERE to access tools to help you quit today.

substance_noaction.pdf_box: When you're ready, learn about resources to support you.

full.text: You chose not to take action on your substance use at this time.
Making those changes can feel challenging, and it's important to take time to think through what feels right for you.
If you decide you'd like support in the future, there are some resources listed below that can help.
HMHI Addiction Recovery Services offers programs for individuals at any stage-whether you're exploring changes or ready to take the next step.
Resources in this toolkit provide practical information and strategies to help you better understand your patterns, explore options for change, and build a plan that fits your goals.
If you're using tobacco, the Regence Empower Program with free nicotine replacement products might be a great place to start.

substance_noansw.pdf_box: Learn more about available resources to support your wellbeing.

full.text: It looks like you may have skipped selecting a resource for substance use.

Substance use can impact both physical health and day-to-day functioning, depending on the type and frequency of use. If you'd like to learn more or explore options, there are resources available to help you better understand next steps.
Learn more about drug and alcohol resources here
Learn more about the Toboacco Quitline here

pcp_has_pcp.name: Yes, has provider
pcp_has_pcp.pdf_box: Schedule with a Primary Care Provider.

full.text: It's great that you have a primary care provider. Seeing your provider annually for a general physical is an important part of maintaining your health and preventing disease. Your provider can also discuss any health concerns with you and recommend appropriate preventive screenings based on your individual needs.
Regular health screenings are essential for early detection and prevention of disease. The specific screenings recommended can vary based on age, gender, family history, and lifestyle factors.
General screenings for most adults include:
Blood pressure checks
Cholesterol levels
Blood sugar tests
Cervical cancer screening
Breast cancer screening
Colon cancer screening
Skin checks
Dental checkups
Immunizations
If it's been a while since your last visit, you may consider scheduling your annual exam. This can be a helpful step in staying on track with your health.

It's important to continue working with your healthcare provider to determine the most appropriate screening schedule for you. They can assess your individual risk factors and provide personalized recommendations.
For more information on health screenings based on age and gender, please visit:
Current national preventative health recommendations
Preventive health services
Cancer screening tests
Vaccines & Immunizations

pcp_connect_pcp.name: No, connect to care
pcp_connect_pcp.pdf_box: Schedule with a Primary Care Provider.

full.text: Establishing care with a primary care provider is an important step in maintaining your health and preventing disease. A provider can help you understand your current health, address any concerns, and recommend appropriate preventive screenings based on your individual needs.
Regular health screenings are essential for early detection and prevention of disease. The specific screenings recommended can vary based on age, gender, family history, and lifestyle factors.
General screenings for most adults include:
Blood pressure checks
Cholesterol levels
Blood sugar tests
Cervical cancer screening
Breast cancer screening
Colon cancer screening
Skin checks
Dental checkups
Immunizations

You indicated that you'd like help getting connected with a provider. The University of Utah Health Care Navigation team will reach out to you with a phone call and MyChart ticket to get scheduled. Your first visit  can help you understand which screenings are most relevant for you and create a plan moving forward.

It's important to consult with a healthcare provider to determine the most appropriate screening schedule for you. They can assess your individual risk factors and provide personalized recommendations.
For more information on health screenings based on age and gender, please visit:
Current national preventative health recommendations
Preventive health services
Cancer screening tests
Vaccines & Immunizations

Establishing care with a primary care provider is an important step in maintaining your health and preventing disease. A provider can help address concerns and recommend appropriate preventive screenings based on your individual needs.
Regular health screenings are essential for early detection. Common screenings for most adults include:
Blood pressure
Cholesterol
Blood sugar
Cervical, breast, and colon cancer screenings
Skin checks
Dental checkups
Immunizations
If you're not ready to see a primary care provider, that's okay. You can still take steps to stay on top of your health.
The Employee RedMed Clinic is a convenient and free option for routine screenings, common concerns, and guidance on next steps.
When you're ready, connecting with a primary care provider can help you stay on track and receive more coordinated care.
For more information:
Current national preventative health recommendations
Preventive health services
Cancer screening tests
Vaccines & Immunizations
